title: allium exile
intro: Allium exile, also known as the wild garlic or field garlic, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Allium family, which includes garlic, onions, and leeks. This plant has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antibacterial and antiviral properties, which make it effective against colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. The health benefits of Allium exile include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health conditions. Herbal preparations of Allium exile include making a tea by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water, as well as using it in cooking and as a natural pest repellent.
